title: Sodium acetate solution
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"i8ib","text":"Sodium acetate solution is used in DNA extraction. This recipe makes 50 mL of a 3 M stock solution at ph 5.2  -- dilute tenfold in water to a working concentration of 0.3 M for use in the DNA extraction protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dissolve 12.3 g of Sodium acetate (anhydrous) in ~ 40 mL MilliQ water. undetermined Sodium acetate anhydrous Contributed by users
Adjust pH to 5.2 with glacial acetic acid
Make up to 50 mL with MilliQ water MilliQ water Contributed by users
Dilute an aliquot of the 3 M stock solution to 0.3 M for use in the DNA extraction protocol.
creator: Andrew Baker
